qa_pairs.question: What is Globus, and what advantages does it offer for researchers transferring files?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus is a service designed for fast, reliable, and secure file transfers, specifically for researchers. It offers an easy-to-use interface with background monitoring and automates the management of file transfers between various resources, including Alliance clusters, other supercomputing facilities, campus clusters, lab servers, desktops, or laptops. Globus improves transfer performance over protocols like GridFTP, rsync, scp, and sftp by automatically tuning transfer settings, restarting interrupted transfers, and checking file integrity, while shielding the end user from complex data movement tasks.

qa_pairs.question: How do I log in to the Alliance Globus portal to access the service?
qa_pairs.answer: To log in to the Alliance Globus portal, go to https://globus.alliancecan.ca/. On the first page, ensure that 'Digital Research Alliance of Canada' is selected in the drop-down box, then click 'Continue'. On the subsequent page, you will be prompted to supply your CCDB username (not your e-mail address or other identifier) and password to access the web portal for Globus.

qa_pairs.question: How do I initiate a file transfer between two collections using the Alliance Globus portal?
qa_pairs.answer: To initiate a file transfer, first navigate to the File Manager page within the Alliance Globus portal. On the top right, click the second 'Panels' button to view two collections simultaneously. Find and select your first collection by typing its name in the search bar (e.g., 'rorqual' to select `alliancecan#rorqual`), and authenticate if prompted. Repeat this process to select your second collection. Once both are active, navigate to the desired directories and highlight the files or directories you wish to transfer. Finally, click one of the large blue buttons with white arrowheads to initiate the transfer. You will receive an email when the transfer is complete, and you can monitor its progress via the 'Activity' button.
